Name: Louis XIV
Analysis: Gold density and elevated heels constructed spectacle-based authority.

Name: Elizabeth I
Analysis: Pearls symbolized purity while spatial collars projected controlled power.

Name: Napoleon Bonaparte
Analysis: Rigid tailoring emphasized discipline and strategic authority.

Name: Cleopatra
Analysis: Gold merged ruler identity with sacred symbolism.

Name: Queen Victoria
Analysis: Sustained mourning reframed monarchy as disciplined and ethical.

Name: Coco Chanel
Analysis: Minimalism shifted power from wealth to autonomy.

Name: Margaret Thatcher
Analysis: Sharp structure translated authority into political presence.

Name: Julius Caesar
Analysis: The laurel served as visual proof of conquest.

Name: Alexander the Great
Analysis: Armor combined intimidation with theatrical presence.

Name: Empress Theodora
Analysis: Rich textiles merged political and religious legitimacy.

Name: Yves Saint Laurent
Analysis: Masculine tailoring disrupted hierarchical gender codes.
